samples.text: A triangle is defined as a shape with exactly three sides. The claim is that this triangle has four sides. Is this claim geometrically consistent?
By definition, a triangle must have precisely three sides. Someone claims this triangle possesses four sides. Does this claim hold up geometrically?
A three-sided polygon is by definition a triangle. The assertion is that this three-sided polygon has four edges. Is this assertion geometrically valid?
This shape is identified as a triangle. Triangles have three sides. The claim attributes four sides to it. Can both the identification and the claim be true simultaneously?
A triangle is defined as a shape with exactly three sides. The claim is that this triangle has four sides. The shape is drawn in red ink. Is this claim geometrically consistent?
